Calling all first year math students📣📣

🎳Come hang out and hit the lanes with your friends!! Whether you’re a strike machine or a proud gutter ball enthusiast, this is your chance to take a break from midterms, meet some new people, and have a great time.

No experience needed, just show up, grab some shoes, and let’s bowl.

signup under the linktree in bio

Your voice matters, and we’re making it easier to be heard. Join students from across Ontario on March 24 to defend accessible education and tell the province to reverse the cuts. By showing up in numbers, we send a clear and unified message to the provincial government: students will not be silent when their futures are on the line.

🎟️ $10 – UW undergrads
🎟️$20 – Non-UW undergrads (i.e. grad students, non-UW students)

This rally is organized by an external student group — WUSA is coordinating transportation to help you get there.

More information can be found at the link in our bio..

Has an instructor made a big impact on you? Nominate them for the Instructor of the Year award! The Instructor of the Year Award has been set up by the Mathematics Society of the University of Waterloo in recognition of the importance of presentation when instructing within the Mathematics Faculty. 

The nomination form can be found on the linktree in bio.

🔺The award is open to only those instructors of full courses within the Mathematics Faculty. Nominations should include a description of the candidate’s teaching technique.🔺

If you have any questions, feel free to email vpa@mathsoc.uwaterloo.ca.

Nominations will close on Friday, March 20th at 11:59 PM.

It’s time for MathSoc’s S26 General Election!

📊 Nominate, campaign, and vote to make your voice heard in shaping MathSoc’s executive team or MathSoc Council 
🗳️ You could become MathSoc’s next President, VP Academic, or represent your program on Council!

All at vote.wusa.ca!!
You have until 11:59 PM on March 4th to nominate yourself!! 

Executive Positions Open:
(1) MathSoc President 
(1) MathSoc VP, Academic

Program Representative Seats Open:
(4) At Large (All Math students)
(2) Actuarial Science (Upper-year ActSci and Math Finance students only)
(3) Statistics (Upper-year Statistics students only)
(3) Pure Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, and Combinatorics and Optimization (Upper-year Pure Math, Applied Math, C&O, Math Finance, and Computational Math students only)
(5) Computer Science (Upper-year Computer Science and Computational Math students only)
(4) Business (Upper-year Math/Business and CS/Business Administration Double Degree students only)
(1) Computing and Financial Management (Upper-year CFM students only)
(1) Teaching (Upper-year Mathematics/Teaching students only)
(4) Mathematical Studies and Other (Upper-year Math Studies students, upper-year undeclared students, and any upper-year students who are not counted in one of the above constituencies)
